$schema_note: Conforms to ADR-025 + ADR-026; inherits shape from cycle_102_d11_hero_variants.json. Image-gen log refs empty (cycle 104 is layout-wiring only; no Imagen calls).

step_1_capture_state.target_surface: site/src/layouts/DocumentationLayout.astro + site/src/pages/{learn,how}/index.astro (wire 2 of 4 hero PNGs produced at cycle 102)
step_1_capture_state.method: agentic discovery via Read tool + md5 fingerprint of pre-change files + grep for existing Astro <Image> + ImageMetadata precedent

pre_change_inventory.documentation_layout_props: { title, description, headings?, prev?, next?, jsonLD? } — heroImage prop absent
section_index_pages_pre_change: site/src/pages/learn/index.astro (44 lines; passes title + description + jsonLD; no hero)
site/src/pages/how/index.astro (39 lines; passes title + description + jsonLD; no hero)
pre_change_inventory.astro_image_usage_in_layouts: absent — no astro:assets Image import anywhere in site/src/{layouts,components/sections}/*.astro pre-cycle-104

step_1_capture_state.scope_decision: Cycle 104 wires the 2 highest-priority section heroes (learn + how — highest-traffic entry surfaces per M5.0 visual inspection methodology) via a pure-additive optional Props extension on DocumentationLayout. Approach: Astro <Image> from astro:assets with responsive widths [640, 960, 1280, 1408] + sizes hint matching the 3-col grid breakpoints (mobile / tablet / desktop) + eager loading (above-fold). Heroes are rendered above <h1> inside .doc-content; aspect-ratio: 16/9 + object-fit: cover. Patterns + reference deferred to cycle 105 (same wiring pattern; no further layout changes).

step_2_persona_qa.method: agent-simulated; persona files re-anchored at session entry. Q&A focus per cycle 104 mission scope = hero PLACEMENT/SIZING/RESPONSIVENESS/A11Y (assets already validated at cycle 102 step_6).
step_2_persona_qa.qa_protocol_focus: Q1 Visual Style (band placement above h1) + Q2 Clarity (sizing + crop behavior at narrow viewports) + Q4 Bloat (load weight; eager vs lazy) + a11y dimension (alt text + role semantics + decorative-vs-informational classification)
step_2_persona_qa.design_question_at_cycle_entry: Where should the hero band sit relative to Breadcrumb + h1? And: should it be eager-loaded (above-fold; LCP impact) or lazy (below-fold safety)?

persona_consensus.design_critic: Above h1, below breadcrumb. Breadcrumb gives location signal first (cognitive anchor); hero confirms section identity; h1 then carries the title weight. Inverting this (hero before breadcrumb) would confuse the user's vertical scan; tucking hero below h1 would weaken the recognition-from-OG-share affordance that cycle 102 designed for.
persona_consensus.visual_designer: 16:9 aspect-ratio + object-fit:cover with border-radius matches the rest of the site's card visual vocabulary. Eager loading on index pages is correct — heroes are clearly above-fold and skipping lazy avoids the layout-shift penalty. Set background to color-bg-alt as a load-state placeholder so the band doesn't collapse before image decodes.
persona_consensus.newcomer_stress_tester: From an OG share to /learn, I want immediate recognition. The hero showing the same paths motif as the OG card I saw is the recognition signal. Eager loading is essential — if the hero pops in late, my first scan misses the confirmation.
persona_consensus.accessibility_auditor: Alt text is required even though the hero is largely decorative; some users will navigate via screen reader and benefit from a brief motif description. Avoid alt="" (which would hide entirely). Use a 1-line description grounded in the motif (e.g., 'Stacked rounded paths motif representing aDNA learning progression') — informational-decorative hybrid. Loading=eager is acceptable here since heroes are above-fold.

pre_change_dimension_aggregate_scores.visual_clarity: N/A — pre-change has no hero rendered in layout (assets exist on disk but not wired)

step_2_persona_qa.qa_synthesis: 4/4 personas converge on: band placement = below breadcrumb, above h1; aspect-ratio = 16/9 with object-fit:cover; loading=eager (above-fold); alt = informational-decorative hybrid (1-line motif description). No regen calls needed — cycle 102 already passed asset validation 4/4 at 4.0-4.5 on Visual_Clarity.

step_3_generate_or_regen_images.decision: NO image generation — cycle 104 is layout-wiring only. Cycle 102 4/4 hero PNGs already pass persona Q&A at asset level (visual_clarity 4.5/explanation_quality 4.0/cognitive_load 4.0 aggregate); regen budget reserved for cycle 105 only if persona Q&A at cycle 105 surfaces a wiring-time concern.

files_modified: site/src/layouts/DocumentationLayout.astro (Props: + heroImage?: { src: ImageMetadata; alt: string }; import: + { Image } from 'astro:assets', + type ImageMetadata; render: <Image src/alt/widths/sizes/loading=eager class=doc-hero/> conditional on heroImage; CSS: + .doc-content :global(.doc-hero) { display block; width 100%; aspect-ratio 16/9; object-fit cover; border-radius var(--radius-md); margin-bottom var(--space-6); background var(--color-bg-alt) })

site/src/pages/learn/index.astro (import heroLearn from ../../assets/heroes/hero_learn.png; pass heroImage={{ src: heroLearn, alt: 'Stacked rounded paths motif representing aDNA learning progression' }})
site/src/pages/how/index.astro (import heroHow from ../../assets/heroes/hero_how.png; pass heroImage={{ src: heroHow, alt: 'Workflow arrows motif representing aDNA operational processes' }})
step_4_implement.implementation_notes: Pure-additive extension. Existing DocumentationLayout consumers (all other section pages + content pages) unaffected — heroImage is optional. Astro <Image> auto-generates 4 responsive webp variants per source (640/960/1280/1408 widths) at build time; original PNG stays as fallback. sizes hint computed against the 3-col grid: desktop = 880px (content column inside 1fr after var(--sidebar-width) and var(--toc-width)); tablet = 100vw - 14rem (sidebar visible, toc hidden); mobile = 100vw.

step_5_recapture.method: Astro production build + image-pipeline log inspection
post_change_artifacts: /_astro/hero_how.Cu0qiCjF_1zg4zG.webp (4 KB) + ZbxXlq (8 KB) + 28Ge95 (11 KB) + Zu6fOG (13 KB) — 4 responsive webp variants for hero_how (640/960/1280/1408w)
/_astro/hero_learn.r9PkPnf9_Z10AFax.webp (8 KB) + ZIrNdj (14 KB) + Z1UdEi2 (20 KB) + 1o2PLC (22 KB) — 4 responsive webp variants for hero_learn
step_5_recapture.build_result: 152 pages built in 5.68s; 18 images optimized in 192ms (12 reused cache + 6 fresh for hero variants); 0 type errors; 0 a11y warnings

step_6_validate.method: Astro production build (npm run build) exits 0 + Astro type-check via @astrojs/check passes + persona post-change scoring on layout-integration quality

persona_specific_post_change_notes.design_critic: Wiring matches the spec: hero band sits below breadcrumb + above h1, 16:9 + rounded corners, eager loaded. Acceptable visual rhythm. The 880px content-width crop on desktop slightly trims the leftmost/rightmost margin of the source — fine for cover-style heroes (no information loss). Score 4.5/5.0 Visual_Clarity.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.visual_designer: Border-radius matches the rest of the card vocabulary. background var(--color-bg-alt) avoids the empty-band flash during decode. Responsive sizes hint correctly avoids serving the 1408px variant to mobile (saves ~18 KB per hero per first-paint). Score 4.5/5.0.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.newcomer_stress_tester: From OG-share → /learn, the matching paths motif lands within the first viewport scan. Recognition signal works as designed. Score 4.0/5.0 Cognitive_Load.
persona_specific_post_change_notes.accessibility_auditor: Alt text is informational-decorative hybrid as discussed; screen readers will surface 1-line motif description. No WCAG violation (decorative imagery permits any alt; non-empty alt assists motif-context users). Score 4.5/5.0 Visual_Clarity.

lift_avoid_verification.m51_d11_lift_1_philosophy_before_feature: HONORED — wiring adds no marketing chrome (no overlay text on hero, no CTA buttons, no aspirational badges); the hero acts as a contextual signal layer, not a sales banner.
lift_avoid_verification.m51_d11_avoid_2_marketing_style_hero_ima: AVOIDED — both wired heroes (learn + how) are abstract geometric motifs (paths, arrows); no human figures, no aspirational photography, no product mockups.

step_6_validate.cycle_acceptance: PASS — DocumentationLayout extended cleanly (additive only; existing consumers unaffected); 2/4 heroes wired (learn + how); build clean at 152 pages 5.68s; responsive image pipeline produces 4 webp variants per hero at /_astro/; persona post-change aggregate 4.0-4.5 across all 4 dimensions; LIFT-1 honored + AVOID-2 avoided.

step_7_record_iii_result.image_gen_log_companion: n/a — cycle 104 had 0 image-gen calls (layout-wiring only)
step_7_record_iii_result.schema_conformance_adr_025_026: Conforms; inherits cycle 102 shape with image_gen_log_refs[] empty (1st canonical instance of layout-wiring cycle JSON in v8 P5; cycles 105+106 inherit this no-image-gen shape).

lift_carried_forward_to_cycle_105: Pure-additive Props extension pattern is reusable for patterns + reference heroes (cycle 105) without further layout changes
responsive sizes hint '(min-width: 1280px) 880px, (min-width: 768px) calc(100vw - 14rem), 100vw' fits the 3-col grid exactly; reuse verbatim at cycle 105
lift_carried_forward_to_cycle_106: import-asset + pass-as-prop pattern can apply to icons-in-band wiring decision (deferred to cycle 106 Step-4 implement based on Q&A)
background: var(--color-bg-alt) load-placeholder discipline works for icons-in-band too
avoid_carried_forward: do NOT lazy-load above-fold heroes (LCP penalty + recognition-signal-from-OG-share weakens)
do NOT use alt='' on heroes (screen reader users lose motif context; decorative-but-informational hybrid is the right shape)
lift_avoid_routing.skill_documentation_layout_props_additiv: NEW SEED candidate 1/3 — pattern: extend a layout's Props with an optional render-block prop + conditional render + scoped global CSS. Reusable across other shared layouts (HomeLayout, MarketingLayout if/when authored). Promote at 3/3.
